framework_name: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF)

controls.title: Identify and document all security requirements for the organization's software development infrastructures and processes
controls.description: Identify and document all security requirements for the organization's software development infrastructures and processes

controls.title: Identify and document all security requirements for organization-developed software to meet, and maintain the requirements over time
controls.description: Identify and document all security requirements for organization-developed software to meet, and maintain the requirements over time

controls.title: Communicate requirements to all third parties who will provide commercial software components for reuse by the organization's own software
controls.description: Communicate requirements to all third parties who will provide commercial software components for reuse by the organization's own software

controls.title: Create new roles and alter responsibilities for existing roles as needed to encompass all parts of the SSDF
controls.description: Create new roles and alter responsibilities for existing roles as needed to encompass all parts of the SSDF

controls.title: Provide role-based training for all personnel with responsibilities that contribute to secure development
controls.description: Provide role-based training for all personnel with responsibilities that contribute to secure development

controls.title: Obtain upper management or authorizing official commitment to secure development, and convey that commitment to all with development-related roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Obtain upper management or authorizing official commitment to secure development, and convey that commitment to all with development-related roles and responsibilities

controls.title: Specify which tools or tool types must or should be included in each toolchain
controls.description: Specify which tools or tool types must or should be included in each toolchain

controls.title: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains
controls.description: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains

controls.title: Configure tools to generate artifacts of their support of secure software development practices as defined by the organization
controls.description: Configure tools to generate artifacts of their support of secure software development practices as defined by the organization

controls.title: Define criteria for software security checks and track throughout the SDLC
controls.description: Define criteria for software security checks and track throughout the SDLC

controls.title: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc. to gather and safeguard the necessary information in support of the criteria
controls.description: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc. to gather and safeguard the necessary information in support of the criteria

controls.title: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development
controls.description: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development

controls.title: Secure and harden development endpoints (i.e., endpoints for software designers, developers, testers, builders, etc.) to perform development-related tasks using a risk-based approach
controls.description: Secure and harden development endpoints (i.e., endpoints for software designers, developers, testers, builders, etc.) to perform development-related tasks using a risk-based approach

controls.title: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code, and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorized personnel, tools, services, etc. have access
controls.description: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code, and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorized personnel, tools, services, etc. have access

controls.title: Make software integrity verification information available to software acquirers
controls.description: Make software integrity verification information available to software acquirers

controls.title: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data (e.g., integrity verification information, provenance data) to be retained for each software release
controls.description: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data (e.g., integrity verification information, provenance data) to be retained for each software release

controls.title: Collect, safeguard, maintain, and share provenance data for all components of each software release
controls.description: Collect, safeguard, maintain, and share provenance data for all components of each software release

controls.title: Use forms of risk modeling — such as threat modeling, attack modeling, or attack surface mapping — to help assess the security risk for the software
controls.description: Use forms of risk modeling — such as threat modeling, attack modeling, or attack surface mapping — to help assess the security risk for the software

controls.title: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks, and design decisions
controls.description: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks, and design decisions

controls.title: Where appropriate, build in support for using standardized security features and services (e.g., enabling system logging for security events to help with incident detection, using standardized log formats) instead of creating proprietary implementations of security features and services
controls.description: Where appropriate, build in support for using standardized security features and services (e.g., enabling system logging for security events to help with incident detection, using standardized log formats) instead of creating proprietary implementations of security features and services

controls.title: Have a qualified person (or people) who were not involved with the design review the software design to confirm that it meets all of the security requirements and satisfactorily addresses the identified risk information
controls.description: Have a qualified person (or people) who were not involved with the design review the software design to confirm that it meets all of the security requirements and satisfactorily addresses the identified risk information

controls.title: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components (e.g., software libraries, modules, middleware, frameworks) from commercial, open source, and other third-party developers for use by the organization's software
controls.description: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components (e.g., software libraries, modules, middleware, frameworks) from commercial, open source, and other third-party developers for use by the organization's software

controls.title: Create and maintain well-secured software components in-house following SDLC processes to meet common internal software development needs that cannot be better met by third-party software components
controls.description: Create and maintain well-secured software components in-house following SDLC processes to meet common internal software development needs that cannot be better met by third-party software components

controls.title: Verify that acquired commercial, open-source, and all other third-party software components comply with the requirements — as defined by the organization — throughout their life cycles
controls.description: Verify that acquired commercial, open-source, and all other third-party software components comply with the requirements — as defined by the organization — throughout their life cycles

controls.title: Follow all secure coding practices that are appropriate to the development languages and environment to meet the organization's requirements
controls.description: Follow all secure coding practices that are appropriate to the development languages and environment to meet the organization's requirements

controls.title: Use compiler, interpreter, and build tools that offer features to improve executable security
controls.description: Use compiler, interpreter, and build tools that offer features to improve executable security

controls.title: Determine which compiler, interpreter, and build tool features should be used and how each should be configured, then implement and use the approved configurations
controls.description: Determine which compiler, interpreter, and build tool features should be used and how each should be configured, then implement and use the approved configurations

controls.title: Determine whether code review (a person directly looks at the code to find issues) and/or code analysis (tools are used to find issues in code, either in a fully automated way or in conjunction with a person) should be used, as defined by the organization
controls.description: Determine whether code review (a person directly looks at the code to find issues) and/or code analysis (tools are used to find issues in code, either in a fully automated way or in conjunction with a person) should be used, as defined by the organization

controls.title: Perform the code review and/or code analysis based on the organization's secure coding standards, and document and triage all discovered issues and recommended remediations in the development team's workflow or issue tracking system
controls.description: Perform the code review and/or code analysis based on the organization's secure coding standards, and document and triage all discovered issues and recommended remediations in the development team's workflow or issue tracking system

controls.title: Determine whether executable code testing should be performed to find vulnerabilities not already found by previous reviews, analyses, and testing and, if so, which types of testing should be used
controls.description: Determine whether executable code testing should be performed to find vulnerabilities not already found by previous reviews, analyses, and testing and, if so, which types of testing should be used

controls.title: Scope the testing, design the tests, perform the testing, and document the results, including documenting and triaging all discovered issues and recommended remediations in the development team's workflow or issue tracking system
controls.description: Scope the testing, design the tests, perform the testing, and document the results, including documenting and triaging all discovered issues and recommended remediations in the development team's workflow or issue tracking system

controls.title: Define a core set of secure settings, and ensure that it is the default configuration
controls.description: Define a core set of secure settings, and ensure that it is the default configuration

controls.title: Implement the default settings (or groups of default settings, if applicable), document each setting for software administrators, and explain how to operate and use the default settings
controls.description: Implement the default settings (or groups of default settings, if applicable), document each setting for software administrators, and explain how to operate and use the default settings

controls.title: Gather information from software acquirers, users, and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software and third-party components that the software uses, and investigate all credible reports
controls.description: Gather information from software acquirers, users, and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software and third-party components that the software uses, and investigate all credible reports

controls.title: Review, analyze, and/or test the software's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities
controls.description: Review, analyze, and/or test the software's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities

controls.title: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement the roles, responsibilities, and processes needed to support that policy
controls.description: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement the roles, responsibilities, and processes needed to support that policy

controls.title: Analyze each vulnerability to gather sufficient information about risk to plan its remediation or other risk response
controls.description: Analyze each vulnerability to gather sufficient information about risk to plan its remediation or other risk response

controls.title: Plan and implement risk responses for vulnerabilities
controls.description: Plan and implement risk responses for vulnerabilities

controls.title: Analyze identified vulnerabilities to determine their root causes
controls.description: Analyze identified vulnerabilities to determine their root causes

controls.title: Analyze the root causes over time to identify patterns, such as a particular secure coding practice not being followed consistently
controls.description: Analyze the root causes over time to identify patterns, such as a particular secure coding practice not being followed consistently

controls.title: Review the software for similar vulnerabilities to eradicate a class of vulnerabilities, and proactively fix them rather than waiting for external reports
controls.description: Review the software for similar vulnerabilities to eradicate a class of vulnerabilities, and proactively fix them rather than waiting for external reports

controls.title: Review the SDLC process and update it if appropriate to prevent the cause of similar vulnerabilities in the future
controls.description: Review the SDLC process and update it if appropriate to prevent the cause of similar vulnerabilities in the future
